qa_pairs.question: How do I properly end an RStudio session in JupyterLab to release resources?
qa_pairs.answer: To properly end an RStudio session and release resources, you must use the `File > Log Out` menu item on the JupyterLab browser tab. Simply quitting RStudio or closing browser tabs will not release the underlying resources.

qa_pairs.question: Does closing an RStudio browser tab release the associated computing resources?
qa_pairs.answer: No, simply quitting RStudio or closing the RStudio and JupyterHub tabs in your browser will not release the resources (CPU, memory, GPU) nor end the underlying Slurm job.

qa_pairs.question: What is MLflow designed for?
qa_pairs.answer: MLflow is an open-source platform purpose-built to assist machine learning practitioners and teams in handling the complexities of the machine learning process, ensuring that each phase is manageable, traceable, and reproducible throughout the full lifecycle for machine learning projects.

qa_pairs.question: Can I load a different version of MLflow than the default?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, you may use a different version of MLflow by loading a specific `mlflow` module, as a default version is loaded by default.

qa_pairs.question: What is OpenRefine primarily used for?
qa_pairs.answer: OpenRefine is a powerful, free, and open-source tool used to clean up messy data, transform it, extend it to add value, and is commonly used to correct typos in manually collected survey data.

qa_pairs.question: How long does it take for a new OpenRefine session to start?
qa_pairs.answer: For a new session, the OpenRefine session can take up to 3 minutes to complete its startup.

qa_pairs.question: Can I reopen an active OpenRefine session after closing the web browser tab?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, it is possible to reopen an active OpenRefine session after the web browser tab was closed.

qa_pairs.question: When does an OpenRefine session terminate?
qa_pairs.answer: The OpenRefine session will end when the JupyterLab session ends.

qa_pairs.question: What capabilities does Tensorboard provide for machine learning experimentation?
qa_pairs.answer: Tensorboard provides visualization and tooling for machine learning experimentation, enabling tracking experiment metrics like loss and accuracy, visualizing the model graph, projecting embeddings to a lower dimensional space, and much more.

qa_pairs.question: What default desktop environments are available in JupyterLab?
qa_pairs.answer: Two different Desktop environments are available by default: Mate Desktop and XFCE Desktop. Users can choose whichever they prefer, with XFCE yielding a more modern UI and Mate being lighter to use.

qa_pairs.question: How long does a new Desktop session take to start in JupyterLab?
qa_pairs.answer: For a new session, the Desktop session can take up to 3 minutes to complete its startup.

qa_pairs.question: Is it possible to reopen a desktop session if the browser tab is closed?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, it is possible to reopen an active desktop session after the web browser tab was closed.

qa_pairs.question: What happens to the desktop session when the JupyterLab session concludes?
qa_pairs.answer: The desktop session will end when the JupyterLab session ends.

qa_pairs.question: What are the uses of the terminal session in JupyterLab?
qa_pairs.answer: The terminal session in JupyterLab can be useful to run bash commands, submit jobs, edit files, access remote filesystems like `/home`, `/project`, and `/scratch`, and run compute tasks without needing an SSH connection.

qa_pairs.question: How do I copy text in a JupyterLab terminal?
qa_pairs.answer: To copy text in a JupyterLab terminal, you should select the text and then press Ctrl+C.

qa_pairs.question: What is the procedure to send a SIGINT signal (Ctrl+C) in a JupyterLab terminal without copying text?
qa_pairs.answer: To send a SIGINT signal or cancel the current command (which usually uses Ctrl+C), you must first click on the terminal to deselect any text before pressing Ctrl+C.

qa_pairs.question: How do you paste text within a JupyterLab terminal?
qa_pairs.answer: To paste text in a JupyterLab terminal, you press Ctrl+V.

qa_pairs.question: What functionality does the Globus icon provide in JupyterLab?
qa_pairs.answer: If Globus is available on the cluster you are using, clicking its icon will open your browser to the corresponding Globus collection.

qa_pairs.question: What does the Metrix launcher icon do in JupyterLab?
qa_pairs.answer: If the Metrix job portal is available on the cluster you are using, the Metrix icon will open a page with the statistics of your job.
